311:     /// Walk up from a branch to trunk, returning the path (branch first, trunk last).
312:     pub fn path_to_trunk(&self, branch: &str) -> Vec<String> {
313:         let mut path = vec![branch.to_string()];
314:         let mut current = branch.to_string();
315:         let mut visited = std::collections::HashSet::new();
316:         visited.insert(branch.to_string());
317:         loop {
318:             if self.is_trunk(&current) {
319:                 break;
320:             }
321:             match self.branches.get(&current) {
322:                 Some(meta) => {
323:                     if !visited.insert(meta.parent.clone()) {
324:                         break; // cycle detected
325:                     }
326:                     path.push(meta.parent.clone());
327:                     current = meta.parent.clone();
328:                 }
329:                 None => break,
330:             }
331:         }
332:         path
333:     }

335:     /// Find the bottom branch (closest to trunk) in the stack containing `branch`.
336:     pub fn stack_bottom(&self, branch: &str) -> String {
337:         let path = self.path_to_trunk(branch);
338:         // path is [branch, ..., trunk], second to last is bottom
339:         if path.len() >= 2 {
340:             path[path.len() - 2].clone()
341:         } else {
342:             branch.to_string()
343:         }
344:     }
345: 
346:     /// Find the top branch (furthest from trunk) by following the first child repeatedly.
347:     pub fn stack_top(&self, branch: &str) -> String {
348:         let mut current = branch.to_string();
349:         let mut visited = std::collections::HashSet::new();
350:         visited.insert(branch.to_string());
351:         loop {
352:             let children = self.children_of(&current);
353:             if children.is_empty() {
354:                 return current;
355:             }
356:             let next = children[0].clone();
357:             if !visited.insert(next.clone()) {
358:                 return current; // cycle detected
359:             }
360:             current = next;
361:         }
362:     }

364:     /// Return the current linear stack as bottom-to-top branch names.
365:     ///
366:     /// This includes the branch's ancestors down to the bottom branch plus any
367:     /// unique child chain above it. If the upward direction branches, the
368:     /// caller must choose a specific tip branch instead.
369:     pub fn linear_stack(&self, branch: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>> {
370:         let mut chain: Vec<String> = self
371:             .path_to_trunk(branch)
372:             .into_iter()
373:             .rev()
374:             .filter(|name| !self.is_trunk(name))
375:             .collect();
376:

378:         loop {

389:                         "`ez merge --stack` is ambiguous from `{current}` because it has multiple child branches: {listed}\n  → Run the command from a specific tip branch, or merge bottom PRs one at a time"
